commands.description: Execute a raw SQL query through Doctrine to verify the exact SQL being generated for a specific platform.
Compare current entity mappings against the database schema and automatically generate a migration file to bridge the gap.

commands.command: $query = $entityManager->createQuery('SELECT u FROM App\Entity\User u WHERE u.id = :id')->setParameter('id', $id);
commands.description: Use DQL (Doctrine Query Language) for object-oriented queries that remain database-agnostic while allowing complex joins.
Detach all loaded entities from the Unit of Work to free up memory and prevent stale data during large batch processing loops.
Check if your current database schema matches your entity mapping definitions to catch synchronization issues early.
